seo.pageTitle: Jeju Island Private Car Charter Tour (9hr) | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: 9-hour fully customizable Jeju private car charter with licensed EN-speaking driver-guide. Pick East (Seongsan, Manjanggul), West (Hyeopjae), or South route.
seo.title: 济州私人包车 · 自选区域一日游 · 9小时客制行程 | AtoC Korea
seo.description: 济州岛私人包车一日游：东部、西部或南部路线自由选择，景点完全客制。含持照英语导游司机，无陌生旅客同乘，饭店接送，全年开团。

catalog_card.title: 济州岛私人包车一日游
catalog_card.subtitle: Design your own Jeju day with a licensed driver-guide and an air-conditioned private vehicle.
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: A 9-hour private car charter for Jeju with a licensed driver-guide. Three recommended routes (East, West, South) cover the island's most-loved sights, or build your own from UNESCO landmarks, K-drama spots, beaches, tea fields, and traditional markets. Hotel pickup is included anywhere on the island.

price.priceNote: 私人包车服务（9小时，含持照英语司机导游）。费用按车辆计算（不按人头）。入场费和餐费另计。AtoC Korea直接销售；相较于运营商底价享有10%折扣。

hero.tagline: 从城山日出峰到协才海滩，到天帝渊瀑布——9小时私人包车，东西南三区任选，行程完全由您决定

itineraryStops.name: 东部路线：UNESCO与韩剧海岸
itineraryStops.description: **济州岛东部路线**是私人包车行程的核心路线选项，以**UNESCO世界遗产城山日出峰**和著名的**韩剧拍摄地涉地可支岬角**为双核心。东部路线全日涵盖：城山日出峰（UNESCO三重认定）登顶或峰下海女表演；涉地可支岬角（安藤忠雄建筑 + 春季油菜花 + 夏季绣球花）；城邑民俗村（朝鲜时代保存城镇）；以及沿东部海岸的咖啡厅停靠。此路线是4条私人包车路线中**摄影景点密度最高**的路线，适合首次造访济州且希望在一日内览遍东部精华的旅客。行程依您的节奏灵活调整，司机导游会根据天气和人潮推荐最佳停靠时机。
highlights: 城山日出峰（UNESCO三重认定）— 登顶或峰下15:00海女表演
涉地可支岬角（安藤忠雄建筑 + 韩剧拍摄地）
城邑民俗村（朝鲜时代保存城镇，UNESCO韩国山寺）
东部海岸咖啡厅停靠（드르쿰다 카페 等设计感强咖啡厅）
**摄影景点密度最高的私人包车路线**
春季4月：油菜花；夏季6月：绣球花；全年：城山日出峰
行程依您的节奏灵活调整
9小时私人包车服务；司机导游负责所有安排
适合首次造访济州且希望览遍东部精华的旅客
timeUsed: Hotel pickup and ~70-90 min transfer from Jeju City to the East coast
2-3 main landmark stops (driver-guide flexes order based on Manjanggul status, weather, and ferry timing for Udo)
Lunch break (free choice — typically near Seongsan harbor for haenyeo seafood, or Seongeup for local Jeju black-pork specialties)
Optional Udo ferry crossing (additional ~8,900 KRW ferry fare per person; vehicle stays parked at Seongsan)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **最适合初次造访济州**、希望最大化标志性景点辨识度的路线。城山日出峰提供岛上最具代表性的UNESCO日出峰摄影，万丈窟（开放时）让路线拥有其他济州路线无可取代的地下UNESCO体验，而涉地可支 + 城邑民俗村则补充了韩剧和民俗文化层次。四条路线中**UNESCO密度最高**的选项。

visitBasics.hours: Most stops 09:00-18:00. Manjanggul (when open) closes the first Wednesday of each month and 17:10 is last entry. Seongsan closes the first Monday of each month for protection.
visitBasics.closed: Seongsan: first Monday monthly. Manjanggul: first Wednesday monthly + currently closed for maintenance (re-confirm post-March 2026).
visitBasics.admission: Pay directly at each stop — Seongsan 5,000 KRW, Manjanggul 4,000 KRW, Seopjikoji 4,000 KRW, Udo round-trip ferry from ~8,900 KRW. Driver-guide assists with ticket purchase.
visitBasics.walking: Moderate to high — Seongsan summit climb is the most demanding section (~30-40 min round trip on stairs). Base-area viewing without the climb is also valid for low-mobility travelers.

convenience.parking: Driver-guide handles all parking — rental-car restrictions on Udo are not a concern for charter passengers (vehicle stays at Seongsan Port)

smartNotes.photo: Seongsan summit at clear-weather morning is the strongest single photograph of the day; Seopjikoji's coastal cape works best in afternoon side-light. For Manjanggul (when open), the world's-tallest lava column at the end of the tourist section is the iconic shot.
smartNotes.facilities: Seongsan harbor and Hamdeok Beach are the strongest meal hubs; the East route has materially stronger food density than the West route. Haenyeo-prepared raw fish is the local seafood signature.
smartNotes.tip: The Seongsan summit climb is the route's bottleneck for senior or low-mobility travelers — base-area viewing is also valid. If Manjanggul is still closed at booking time, the driver-guide will substitute Sangumburi Crater or Hamdeok Beach. Time the Udo ferry at least 4 hours before sunset to be safe.

_poi_meta.note: Route option for private charter — represents a multi-POI bundle (Seongsan, Udo, Manjanggul, Seopjikoji, Seongeup, Hamdeok); not a single matchable POI. All constituent POIs are individually verified in KB v1.10.

itineraryStops.name: 西部路线：海滩、茶园与咖啡厅海岸
itineraryStops.description: **济州岛西部路线**是私人包车行程的休闲路线选项，以**挟才海滩的翡翠海水**、**雪绿茶博物馆的壮观茶园**和**爱月咖啡厅街的日落海景**为三大核心。西部路线全日涵盖：挟才海滩（济州西海岸前三大，银白色沙滩 + 飞扬岛景观）；翰林公园（9个主题花园，每月有命名节庆）；雪绿茶博物馆 + 茶园步道（爱茉莉太平洋旗舰，免费入场）；爱月咖啡厅街（春秋季日落17:30至18:30的最佳观赏地）；以及协在悦诗风吟济州之家。此路线是4条私人包车路线中**最适合放松节奏**的选项，适合偏好海岸风光和咖啡厅文化而非密集景点打卡的旅客。
highlights: 挟才海滩 — 济州西海岸前三大，银白色沙滩 + 飞扬岛景观
翰林公园 — 9个主题花园，每月有命名节庆（含挟才窟熔岩管洞穴）
雪绿茶博物馆 + 茶园步道（爱茉莉太平洋旗舰，免费入场）
爱月咖啡厅街 — 春秋季日落最佳观赏地（17:30至18:30）
悦诗风吟济州之家（免费入场，可选柚子皂DIY）
**最适合放松节奏的私人包车路线**
春季3至4月：油菜花；夏季6月：绣球花；全年：茶园和海滩
9小时私人包车服务；行程依您的节奏灵活调整
适合偏好海岸风光和咖啡厅文化的旅客
timeUsed: Hotel pickup and ~50-70 min transfer to the West coast
Morning beach + Hallim Park (with optional cave-visit detour inside the park)
Lunch break (Aewol abalone porridge, Hyeopjae area haenyeo seafood, or Daejeong-eup Korean BBQ)
Afternoon O'sulloc + adjacent Innisfree Jeju House, then late-afternoon coffee in Aewol
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **最适合偏好悠闲节奏**、以摄影背景和咖啡厅文化为优先的旅客，而非UNESCO清单打卡。步行量明显少于东部路线，室内备选密度高（翰林公园溶洞 + 温室、雪绿茶 + 悦诗风吟），晴天或局部多云的天气都同样适合。

visitBasics.hours: Most stops 09:00-18:00; Aewol cafés typically 10:00-22:00 with last-order ~21:30
visitBasics.closed: Most stops open year-round; individual cafés close on rotating weekdays
visitBasics.admission: Hyeopjae Beach free; Hallim Park ~12,000 KRW adult; O'sulloc Tea Museum free; Aewol cafés pay-per-order. Driver-guide assists with park ticket purchase.
visitBasics.walking: Low-to-moderate — beach walks, flat park paths, café-to-café strolls; suitable for all ages and mobility levels

convenience.restroom: Available at all major stops; cafés have facilities for paying customers
convenience.parking: Driver-guide handles all parking; Aewol street parking is tight on weekends — private charter solves this

smartNotes.photo: Hyeopjae at low tide with Biyangdo behind is the iconic shot; O'sulloc tea-field rows photograph best in morning when light is soft; Aewol café terraces at golden hour close the day strongly.
smartNotes.facilities: Aewol has the strongest café density on Jeju — beat-the-crowd tip is to lock in your sunset café reservation in the morning so the slot is held. Hallim Park's interior garden cafe is a strong rainy-day option.
smartNotes.tip: If your itinerary needs a single weather-flexible Jeju day, this is the route — Hallim Park caves and the O'sulloc indoor museum cover ~3 hours of indoor time on a rainy day. Best paired with a sunset Aewol café finish; reserve cafés in the morning.

_poi_meta.note: Route option for private charter — represents a multi-POI bundle (Hyeopjae, Hallim, O'sulloc, Aewol); not a single matchable POI. All constituent POIs verified in KB v1.10.

itineraryStops.name: 南部路线：瀑布与火山海岸
itineraryStops.description: **济州岛南部路线**是私人包车行程的地质景观路线选项，以**正房瀑布（亚洲唯一直接落入大海的瀑布）**、**大浦柱状节理海岸（东亚最具代表性的柱状节理）**和**天帝渊瀑布（三层瀑布 + 七仙女传说）**为三大核心，同时造访**汉拏山御乘生岳**和**O'sulloc雪绿茶博物馆**。南部路线是4条私人包车路线中**地质多样性最高**的选项，一日之内涵盖山岳、海岸悬崖、瀑布、茶园等截然不同的地景。适合对济州地质和自然景观有兴趣的旅客，以及希望在一日内尽览济州南部精华的旅客。行程依您的节奏灵活调整。
highlights: 正房瀑布 — 亚洲唯一直接落入大海的瀑布（垂直落下23公尺）
大浦柱状节理海岸 — 东亚最具代表性的柱状节理（天然纪念物第443号）
天帝渊瀑布 — 三层瀑布 + 仙临桥七仙女传说
汉拏山御乘生岳 — UNESCO三重认定，1小时来回步道（免费入场）
O'sulloc雪绿茶博物馆 — 免费入场，茶园步道
**地质多样性最高的私人包车路线**
一日涵盖山岳、海岸悬崖、瀑布、茶园等截然不同地景
行程依您的节奏灵活调整；9小时私人包车服务
适合对济州地质和自然景观有兴趣的旅客
timeUsed: Hotel pickup and ~60-90 min transfer to the South coast (Seogwipo)
Morning waterfall + basalt-cliff stops (Cheonjiyeon then Jusangjeolli)
Lunch break in Seogwipo (Olle Market street food, or Jungmun area for sit-down meal)
Afternoon Sanbangsan or Yakcheonsa + market browse before return
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **最适合希望在一天内体验地质多样性**的旅客——寺庙、瀑布、玄武岩悬崖、火山丘，以及传统市场，涵盖了济州「火山岛」身份认同的标志性元素。同时也是三条路线中**室内备选密度最高**的路线（药泉寺主法堂、奥勒市场有顶arcade、山房窟寺洞穴）——使其成为天气最灵活的选择。

visitBasics.hours: Most stops 09:00-17:30. Cheonjiyeon illuminated 09:00-22:00. Olle Market: 07:00-21:00 summer, 07:00-20:00 winter; night market 18:00-24:00 daily.
visitBasics.closed: Most stops open year-round; individual market shops close on rotating weekdays
visitBasics.admission: Cheonjiyeon 2,000 KRW; Jusangjeolli ~2,000 KRW; Sanbanggulsa ~1,000 KRW; Yakcheonsa free; Olle Market free entry. Driver-guide assists with tickets.
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — Cheonjiyeon trail flat 1 km, Sanbangsan stairs 0.6 mi to cave temple, market browse on flat covered arcade. Sanbangsan stairs are the bottleneck for low-mobility travelers.

convenience.restroom: Available at all major stops; market has multiple
convenience.parking: Driver-guide handles all parking; Olle Market parking can be tight at weekends — private charter avoids this

smartNotes.photo: Cheonjiyeon's evening illumination is the unique shot (no other major Jeju waterfall illuminates nightly); Sanbangsan with spring rapeseed at the base is the springtime signature; Jusangjeolli's hexagons in late-afternoon side-light show the column geometry best.
smartNotes.facilities: Olle Market is the strongest single food stop on Jeju — Jeseong Bakery's octopus buns, mochi-pancakes, and Hallabong-juice stalls. The black-pork restaurants near Sanbangsan/Daejeong are also strong. Yakcheonsa's templestay vegetarian meal is bookable separately.
smartNotes.tip: For a rainy day on Jeju, this is the strongest of the three named routes — Yakcheonsa main hall, Sanbanggulsa cave, and Olle Market covered arcade together cover ~3-4 hours of indoor time. The Sanbangsan summit itself is closed; the cave temple at 150 m is the visitor endpoint.

_poi_meta.note: Route option for private charter — represents a multi-POI bundle (Cheonjiyeon, Jusangjeolli, Sanbangsan, Yakcheonsa, Jeongbang, Olle Market); not a single matchable POI. All constituent POIs verified in KB v1.10.

itineraryStops.name: 客制路线：打造您的专属行程
itineraryStops.description: **客制路线**是济州岛私人包车行程的完全自由选项——司机导游依照**您自行选择的景点清单**，安排全日9小时的个人化济州之旅。您可以选择任何组合：UNESCO遗产景点（城山日出峰、汉拏山御乘生岳）、南部海岸（正房瀑布、柱状节理）、西部海岸（挟才海滩、雪绿茶博物馆）、东部海岸（涉地可支、城邑民俗村）、特色体验（橘子采摘、牛岛渡轮、海女表演）、咖啡厅和购物，或任何您希望探索的地点。司机导游会提供景点之间的最佳路线建议，并根据您的节奏和天气状况灵活调整。9小时私人包车服务，费用根据车型和团体人数而定；所有入场费和餐费另计。
highlights: 完全自由的9小时个人化济州之旅
自行选择任何景点的组合
UNESCO遗产、南部海岸、西部海岸、东部海岸、特色体验任选
司机导游提供景点之间的最佳路线建议
根据您的节奏和天气状况灵活调整
**9小时私人包车服务**；费用根据车型和团体人数而定
入场费和餐费另计
特色体验可选：橘子采摘、牛岛渡轮、海女表演
适合有特定兴趣或曾多次到访济州的旅客
timeUsed: Discuss your plan with the driver-guide at pickup or send it 24 hours ahead via the booking confirmation
Driver-guide sequences stops based on real-time traffic, ferry schedules, and individual stop closure days (Seongsan first Monday, Manjanggul first Wednesday, etc.)
Return to your hotel within the 9-hour window (overage by prior agreement)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 四条路线中**最灵活的选项**。最适合多次造访济州的旅客、有特殊兴趣（韩剧追踪、摄影、美食、携带儿童的家庭）的旅客，以及4至9人团体（按车辆计费的私人包车在对比小型团体游时，费用天平明显倾向私人包车）。

visitBasics.hours: Depends on chosen stops — driver-guide flags monthly closure days at booking
visitBasics.admission: All admission fees pay directly; driver-guide assists at each gate

smartNotes.photo: Send 3-5 reference photos to the driver-guide in advance — it dramatically improves the quality of your final shot list
smartNotes.facilities: Lunch is your call — communicate dietary needs (halal, vegetarian, no-seafood, kids' menu) at booking so the guide can suggest appropriate restaurants
smartNotes.tip: The custom route is also the best fit for visitors with a flight that day — the 9-hour window can be back-loaded to drop you at Jeju Airport at the end. State this at booking so the route is sequenced toward the airport in the late afternoon.

_poi_meta.note: Fully customizable route option — candidate POIs above are verified in KB v1.10 but not prescribed; final stop list determined by passenger's request at booking.

routeShapeIntro.title: 行程动线总览
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: Pickup at your Jeju hotel between 08:00 and 09:00, then a fully flexible 9-hour day around the East, West, or South route—or your own custom plan. Driver-guide adjusts pacing based on weather and traffic. Return drop-off around 17:00 to 18:00.

bestFor: 携带幼龄儿童（包括婴儿）的家庭
包含长辈的多代同行团体
追寻悠闲韩剧氛围一日游的情侣和蜜月旅客
希望客制化路线的多次访济旅客
追寻特定季节景观的摄影爱好者（油菜花、绣球花、粉黛乱子草）

routeLogicSections.title: 为什么私人包车在济州最有优势

items.label: 济州岛地理上确实广大
items.detail: 济州岛东西跨距约73公里。团体巴士游在每个景点都要等待；私人包车省去这些时间，让每个景点都有更充裕的停留时间。

items.label: 天气变化极快
items.detail: 济州小气候每小时都在变化。司机导游可以将户外景点替换为室内景点（翰林公园溶洞、雪绿茶、药泉寺），而不会破坏整体行程。

items.label: 门到门的舒适体验
items.detail: 饭店接送意味著不需要机场区接驳、不需要深夜巴士的压力，也不需要携带行李在换乘点间步行。

routeLogicSections.title: 如何在东部、西部和南部路线之间选择

items.label: 东部路线——初次访客最大化地标辨识度
items.detail: 城山日出峰和万丈窟是济州两个最强的UNESCO知名景点；东部路线提供最高的标志性识别度。

items.label: 西部路线——轻松的风景和摄影体验
items.detail: 挟才海滩、翰林公园、雪绿茶和爱月构成更柔和、更适合情侣的一日游，摄影收益强，以日落结尾。

items.label: 南部路线——地质奇观的震撼感
items.detail: 瀑布、玄武岩悬崖、火山丘和寺庙集中在一天中，让这条路线具有济州最强烈的「火山岛」叙事主轴。

items.label: 天气调整
items.detail: 小雨很少影响路线；强风通常缩短沿海景点的停留时间（城山日出峰、柱状节理、爱月）。

items.label: 东部路线的牛岛渡轮
items.detail: 牛岛需要另行乘渡轮。车辆可以一起渡海（额外费用）或停在码头。渡轮时间安排影响全天整体节奏。

items.label: 加时服务可安排
items.detail: 若需要超过9小时的服务，每小时加时费用为25,000韩圜，现金支付给司机，视司机可用时间而定。

lessIdealFor: 独行的预算旅客（私人包车按车辆计费）
希望与其他旅客共同旅行、享受社交体验的旅客
需要全程官方认证导游深度解说的旅客（司机导游持照但非全程导览等级的解说）
希望入场费和餐费全包的旅客

practicalAccordionItems.title: 接送与下车地点
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 济州任一饭店接送；大厅提前10分钟等候
content: 接送服务涵盖济州岛任一饭店（济州市、西归浦、中文等）
接送时间通常安排在08:00至09:00之间
请在约定出发时间前10分钟在饭店大厅等候
下车地点可为您的济州饭店或岛上任一地点（如晚餐餐厅或机场）
回程通常在9小时服务时段内的17:00至18:00之间
如需改期，请至少提前2天联系运营商

practicalAccordionItems.title: 车辆与人数限制
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 车型依团体人数和行李量分配
content: 车型依精确乘客人数和行李件数匹配
预约时请填写精确的乘客人数和行李件数
典型车型：1至3人轿车、4至5人中型SUV、6至9人Staria或Carnival厢型车
较大的团体可能需要分乘多辆车辆或另行报价

practicalAccordionItems.title: 9小时行程与延时收费
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 9小时严格服务时段，从接送时间计算
content: 9小时服务时段从饭店接送时间开始计算
加时收费：每额外小时25,000韩圜，现金支付给司机
加时视司机可用时间而定，并受服务时数规定限制
牛岛渡轮的往返时间计入9小时服务时段内（除非另有协议）

practicalAccordionItems.title: 需知额外费用
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 牛岛渡轮、入场费和餐费须知
content: 牛岛车辆渡轮：另行收费（依车型而异）；步行旅客渡轮约₩10,500来回/人
所有景点入场费在现场直接支付
餐费直接支付；司机导游可推荐当地特色料理（济州黑猪、猪骨汤面、鲍鱼粥）
可选司机小费感谢，但非强制

practicalAccordionItems.title: 天候政策
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 私人包车可灵活应对济州天气变化
content: 小雨：大多数景点照常进行；司机导游可视情况调整至室内景点优先
强风：沿海景点如城山日出峰、柱状节理和爱月可能缩短停留
台风警报日：运营商将主动联系您协商改期
汉拏山降雪可能关闭1100公路；司机导游将以替代内陆景点替换

practicalAccordionItems.title: 费用包含项目
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 包含车辆和持照司机导游；入场费和餐费自付
content: 包含：私人车辆（依团体人数为轿车、SUV或厢型车）
包含：持照英语司机导游
包含：济州岛任一饭店接送
包含：标准路线的燃油费和过路费
不包含：各景点入场费（现场直接支付）
不包含：餐费
不包含：个人购物费用
不包含：司机小费

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: 济州岛全年气候温和，四条私人包车路线均可全年运行。天气在各地区之间可能有差异——沿海景点全年气候温和，但汉拏山高地路段可能较为凉爽或有强风。台风季节（7至9月）偶有强风影响部分景点。

seasonalVariations.title: Canola season
seasonalVariations.description: 3月下旬至4月东部海岸的油菜花和赏花同时盛开；4月初进入赏花高峰。气温温和、天空明朗，是四条路线最均衡的季节。

seasonalVariations.title: Beach season
seasonalVariations.description: 挟才和咸德海滩进入海水浴高峰，6月绣球花为西部海岸增添色彩。预计较高的湿度，8月可能有台风天气。

seasonalVariations.title: Best photo season
seasonalVariations.description: 10月中旬至11月初的粉黛乱子草达到高峰；这是欣赏汉拏山和城山视野最清晰的视窗，通常是济州最佳摄影季节。

seasonalVariations.title: Camellia & snow
seasonalVariations.description: 12月至2月山茶花丘进入盛花期；汉拏山峰顶可能积雪。天气寒冷但视觉效果绝佳，各主要景点游客人数最少。

staticQuestions.question: 这个行程适合初次造访济州的旅客吗（东部、西部或南部路线）？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的——每条路线都涵盖了该区域最具特色的景点，在一天的均衡行程中完整呈现。

staticQuestions.question: 步行难度如何？
staticQuestions.answer: 整体中等难度，含部分较为活跃的步行段落。

staticQuestions.question: 这个行程适合携带儿童的家庭吗？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的——对大多数家庭均舒适可行。

staticQuestions.question: 年长旅客参加是否方便？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的——行程节奏和步行量对体力较佳的长辈均舒适可行。

meeting_points.name: 济州岛任一饭店（济州市、西归浦、中文）
meeting_points.note: Door-to-door pickup at any hotel within the supported area.

meeting_points.name: 济州国际机场
meeting_points.note: Jeju Airport pickup also available; counts inside the 9-hour window.

dropoff_points.name: Any Jeju-island hotel (Jeju City, Seogwipo, Jungmun)
dropoff_points.note: Drop-off at the same hotel or any address within the supported area.

dropoff_points.name: Jeju Airport
dropoff_points.note: Same-day departure drop-off available; counts inside the service window.

pickup_dropoff.notes: 请在约定出发时间前10分钟在饭店大厅等候。9小时服务时段从饭店接送时间开始计算。改期请求应在原预约日期前至少2天提出。加时收费为每小时25,000韩圜，现金支付给司机，视司机可用时间而定。

walking_notes: Walking load depends entirely on the chosen route and stops.
East route: Seongsan summit climb is the most demanding section; base-area viewing is an easier alternative.
West route: easy to moderate overall; Hyeopjae Beach and O'sulloc are gentle.
South route: Cheonjiyeon and Sanbangsan add the most stairs.
The driver-guide can adjust pacing on the day to match family or senior comfort.
Comfortable walking shoes with good grip are recommended.
synonym_hints: jeju door to door private tour

matching_metadata.note: Scores were tuned for a fully customizable 9-hour Jeju private charter format. Comfort, mobility-friendliness, family fit, and senior fit score very high because the private format eliminates group constraints and the driver-guide handles all transit. Same-day-flight fit scores 4 because Jeju Airport is centrally located and the driver-guide can flex the route around flight times. Walking and culture levels are weighted averages across the three recommended routes; the on-the-day load can move significantly depending on the chosen route.

score_rationale.private_fit: Direct match: this is a per-vehicle private charter.
score_rationale.mobility_friendly_fit: Door-to-door private vehicle eliminates most public-transport mobility friction; only Seongsan summit and Cheonjiyeon stairs reduce friendliness for those specific stops.
score_rationale.young_kids_fit: Best-in-class for kids because pacing, snack stops, and route choice are all adjustable.
score_rationale.weather_sensitivity: Low because the driver-guide can shift toward indoor stops (Manjanggul, Hallim caves, O'sulloc, Yakcheonsa) on bad-weather days.
score_rationale.value_for_money_fit: Mid: per-vehicle pricing is excellent value for groups of 4 to 9 but expensive for solo travelers.
score_rationale.iconic_landmark_fit: Maxed because the route can include Seongsan, Manjanggul, Cheonjiyeon, and Jusangjeolli in any combination.
score_rationale.same_day_flight_fit: Above average because Jeju Airport is central and the driver-guide can drop off there at the end of the 9 hours.

hero.tagline: From Seongsan Sunrise Peak to Hyeopjae Beach to Cheonjiyeon Falls—nine private hours, your route, your pace, with a licensed driver-guide.

galleryItems.caption: Jusangjeolli basalt cliff
galleryItems.atmosphere: Hexagonal basalt columns on the South coast

galleryItems.caption: Hyeopjae Beach West Jeju
galleryItems.atmosphere: Emerald water and white sand on the West coast

galleryItems.caption: O'sulloc tea museum and fields
galleryItems.atmosphere: Green tea-field rows and a calming midday reset

galleryItems.caption: Cheonjiyeon waterfall in Seogwipo
galleryItems.atmosphere: Forest waterfall on the South route

itineraryStops.name: East Route: UNESCO & K-Drama Coast
itineraryStops.description: The most landmark-dense route option, covering Jeju's strongest UNESCO and K-drama spots. Typical stops include Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO sunrise peak), Udo Island (ferry-access island with beaches), Manjanggul Lava Tube (UNESCO cave), Seopjikoji (drama-famous coastal cape), Seongeup Folk Village, and Hamdeok Beach.
highlights: Seongsan Ilchulbong: UNESCO World Natural Heritage and Jeju's flagship sunrise peak
Udo Island: ferry-access island with white-sand beaches and lighthouse views
Manjanggul: one of the world's longest lava-tube caves (UNESCO)
Seopjikoji and Seongeup: classic K-drama and folk-village atmosphere
timeUsed: Hotel pickup and transfer to the East coast
2 to 3 main landmark stops (driver-guide adjusts based on group pace)
Lunch break (free choice; usually near Seongsan or in the East coast)
Optional Udo ferry crossing (extra cost; vehicle parked or shipped)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It is the strongest route for first-time Jeju visitors who want maximum recognition: Seongsan Ilchulbong is the island's single most-photographed landmark, and Manjanggul gives the route a distinct UNESCO underground experience.

visitBasics.hours: Most stops 09:00 to 18:00; Manjanggul closes the first Wednesday of each month
visitBasics.closed: Seongsan closed first Monday of each month; Manjanggul closed first Wednesday
visitBasics.admission: Pay directly: Seongsan 5,000 KRW, Manjanggul 4,000 KRW, Seopjikoji 4,000 KRW, Udo ferry from 10,500 KRW round-trip
visitBasics.walking: Moderate to high (Seongsan summit climb is the most demanding section)

smartNotes.photo: Seongsan summit at clear-weather morning is the strongest single photo on the route; Seopjikoji works better in afternoon side-light.
smartNotes.facilities: Seongsan and Hamdeok Beach are the strongest meal hubs; the East route has stronger food density than the West.
smartNotes.tip: The Seongsan summit climb is the route's bottleneck for senior or low-mobility travelers. Base-area viewing also works.

_poi_meta.note: Route option for private charter — represents a multi-POI bundle (Seongsan, Udo, Manjanggul, Seopjikoji); not a single matchable POI

itineraryStops.name: West Route: Beaches, Tea & Cafe Coast
itineraryStops.description: The most photogenic and relaxed route. Typical stops include Hyeopjae Beach (Jeju's most iconic emerald-water beach), Hallim Park (subtropical gardens and lava caves), O'sulloc Tea Museum (tea fields and cafe), and Aewol Cafe Street (sunset coastal cafes). Strong fit for couples and photographers.
highlights: Hyeopjae Beach: emerald water, white sand, and Biyangdo island views
Hallim Park: subtropical garden with two on-site lava caves
O'sulloc: Jeju's most famous tea museum, with strong indoor support
Aewol Cafe Street: sunset coast and contemporary Jeju cafe culture
timeUsed: Hotel pickup and transfer to the West coast
Beach and garden stops in the morning
Lunch break (free choice; strongest options near Hallim or Hyeopjae)
Tea museum and Aewol close to finish the day
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It is the strongest route for couples, photographers, and travelers wanting a softer pace. Aewol works particularly well as a sunset close, and O'sulloc gives the route a useful weather-buffer indoor section.

visitBasics.hours: Most stops 09:00 to 18:00; Aewol cafes vary
visitBasics.admission: Pay directly: Hallim Park 15,000 KRW, O'sulloc free entry (programs cost extra), beaches free

smartNotes.photo: Hyeopjae works best in late-morning sun for emerald-water color; Aewol photographs strongest at sunset.
smartNotes.facilities: O'sulloc has the best indoor support of all the West stops; Aewol has the densest cafe and snack options.
smartNotes.tip: If the day forecast is uncertain, the West route handles light rain better than the East route.

_poi_meta.note: Route option for private charter — multi-POI bundle (Hyeopjae, Hallim, O'sulloc, Aewol)

itineraryStops.name: South Route: Waterfalls & Volcanic Coast
itineraryStops.description: The most scenically dramatic route. Typical stops include Cheonjiyeon Falls (forest waterfall), Jusangjeolli Cliff (basalt-column coastline), Yakcheonsa Temple (one of the largest temples in East Asia), Sanbangsan Mountain (volcanic dome with sea views), Jeongbang Waterfall (one of Asia's few waterfalls flowing directly into the sea), and Seogwipo Olle Market.
highlights: Cheonjiyeon Falls: a classic Seogwipo forest waterfall
Jusangjeolli Cliff: hexagonal basalt columns on the south coast
Sanbangsan: volcanic dome with sea-cliff temple views
Olle Market: Seogwipo's most lively traditional market for snacks and souvenirs
timeUsed: Hotel pickup and transfer to the South coast
Morning waterfall and basalt-cliff stops
Lunch break in Seogwipo (free choice)
Afternoon mountain or temple stop, market close
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It is the strongest route for travelers who want geological variety: a temple, a waterfall, a basalt cliff, a volcanic dome, and a market in one day cover Jeju's strongest 'volcanic island' identity.

visitBasics.admission: Pay directly: Cheonjiyeon 2,500 KRW, Jusangjeolli 2,000 KRW, Jeongbang 2,000 KRW, Yakcheonsa free, Sanbangsan 1,000 KRW
visitBasics.walking: Moderate (Cheonjiyeon and Sanbangsan add the most walking)

smartNotes.photo: Jusangjeolli works best in afternoon side-light; Olle Market is strongest in late afternoon when the night-market section opens.
smartNotes.facilities: Olle Market is the strongest food hub; Yakcheonsa offers the calmest indoor visit.
smartNotes.tip: Cheonjiyeon and Sanbangsan both involve stairs. Travelers with knee issues should pick one rather than both.

_poi_meta.note: Route option for private charter — multi-POI bundle (Cheonjiyeon, Jusangjeolli, Yakcheonsa, Sanbangsan, Jeongbang)

itineraryStops.name: Custom Route: Build Your Own Day
itineraryStops.description: Beyond the three recommended routes, you can mix stops or send your own itinerary. Possible additions include the Jeju Aquarium Planet, ECO Land Theme Park, Sangumburi Crater, Hamdeok Beach, Camellia Hill, Teddy Bear Safari, Seogwipo Submarine (Munseom underwater tour), dolphin yacht tours, Songaksan, Cafe the Cliff, and Nanta show.
highlights: Mix East-coast UNESCO with West-coast tea fields if time permits
Add a haenyeo (female-diver) seafood lunch to any route
Combine a beach morning with a mountain or temple afternoon
Pair with K-drama filming locations specific to your favorite shows
timeUsed: Discuss your plan with the driver-guide at pickup or before
Driver-guide adjusts pacing based on traffic and ferry timing
Return to your hotel within the 9-hour window
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It is the most flexible option of the four. It works best for repeat Jeju visitors, travelers with niche interests (K-drama tracking, photography, food), and families who want a tailored mix.

convenience.restroom: Driver-guide will plan around stop facilities

smartNotes.photo: Custom routes can target specific seasonal photo windows: canola fields in spring, hydrangeas in early summer, pink muhly in autumn.
smartNotes.facilities: Discuss meal timing and rest stops with your driver-guide before departure.
smartNotes.tip: Send your itinerary draft a few days before the tour so the driver-guide can flag any timing or access issues, especially around ferry schedules.

_poi_meta.note: Custom route — fully guest-defined; no fixed POI set

routeShapeIntro.title: How this private day moves
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: Pickup at your Jeju hotel between 08:00 and 09:00, then a fully flexible 9-hour day around the East, West, or South route—or your own custom plan. Driver-guide adjusts pacing based on weather and traffic. Return drop-off around 17:00 to 18:00.

bestFor: Families with children, including very young kids
Couples and honeymooners wanting a relaxed K-drama day
Repeat Jeju visitors who want a tailored route
Photographers chasing specific seasonal scenes (canola, hydrangeas, pink muhly)

routeLogicSections.title: Why a private charter wins on Jeju

items.label: Jeju is genuinely large
items.detail: The island spans about 73 km east-to-west. Group bus tours waste hours waiting at each stop; a private charter cuts that overhead.

items.label: Weather changes fast
items.detail: Jeju microclimates shift hourly. A driver-guide can swap an outdoor stop for an indoor one (Hallim Park caves, O'sulloc, Yakcheonsa) without ruining the day.

items.label: Door-to-door comfort
items.detail: Hotel pickup means no airport-area shuttle, no late-evening bus stress, and no walking with luggage between transfers.

routeLogicSections.title: How to choose between East, West, and South

items.label: East for first-time landmark coverage
items.detail: Seongsan Ilchulbong and Manjanggul are Jeju's two strongest UNESCO names; the East route delivers maximum recognition value.

items.label: West for relaxed scenery and photos
items.detail: Hyeopjae Beach, Hallim Park, O'sulloc, and Aewol form a softer, more couple-friendly day with strong photo payoff and a sunset finish.

items.label: South for geological drama
items.detail: Waterfalls, basalt cliffs, a volcanic dome, and a temple in one day give this route Jeju's strongest 'volcanic island' narrative.

items.label: Weather adjustments
items.detail: Light rain rarely affects the route; strong wind usually shortens coastal stops (Seongsan, Jusangjeolli, Aewol).

items.label: Udo ferry on the East route
items.detail: Udo Island requires a separate ferry. Vehicles can be shipped (extra cost) or left at the wharf. Ferry timing affects your overall day flow.

items.label: Overtime is possible
items.detail: If you need more than 9 hours, an extra hour is 25,000 KRW paid in cash to the driver, subject to driver availability.

lessIdealFor: Solo budget travelers (private pricing is per vehicle)
Travelers wanting to mix with other guests for a social experience
Anyone needing a fully narrated certified-guide tour (driver-guide is licensed but not full guide-grade narration)
Travelers wanting fully included admission and meals

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Hotel pickup anywhere on Jeju; lobby meeting 10 minutes early
content: Hotel pickup is included anywhere on Jeju Island (Jeju City, Seogwipo, Jungmun, etc.)
Pickup is typically arranged between 08:00 and 09:00
Please wait at the hotel lobby 10 minutes before the agreed departure time
Drop-off at your Jeju hotel or any Jeju-island location of your choice (e.g., a dinner restaurant or the airport)
Return is typically between 17:00 and 18:00 within the 9-hour window
If you need to reschedule, please contact the operator at least 2 days before the original reservation date

practicalAccordionItems.title: Vehicle and Group Size
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Vehicle assigned by group size and luggage volume
content: The vehicle type is matched to the exact number of passengers and luggage pieces
Please fill in the exact passenger count and luggage count when booking
Typical vehicles include sedans for 1 to 3 guests, mid-size SUVs for 4 to 5 guests, and Staria or Carnival vans for 6 to 9 guests
Larger groups may be split across multiple vehicles or quoted separately

practicalAccordionItems.title: 9-Hour Window and Overtime
content: The 9-hour window starts from your hotel pickup time
Overtime is charged at 25,000 KRW per additional hour, paid in cash directly to the driver
Overtime is subject to driver availability and applicable hours of service rules
Udo Island ferry crossings count inside the 9-hour window unless otherwise agreed

practicalAccordionItems.title: Extra Fees You Should Know About
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Udo ferry, admission costs, and meals
content: Udo Island vehicle ferry: extra cost (varies by vehicle size); foot-passenger ferry from ≈10,500 KRW round-trip per person
All admission fees are paid directly at each venue
Meals are paid directly; the driver-guide can recommend local specialties (heuk dwaeji black pork, gogi guksu, abalone porridge)
Optional driver gratuities are appreciated but not required

practicalAccordionItems.title: Weather policy
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Private format adapts well to changing Jeju conditions
content: Light rain: most stops still operate; the driver-guide can shift toward indoor stops
Strong wind: coastal exposure at Seongsan, Jusangjeolli, and Aewol may be shortened
Typhoon-warning days: the operator will contact you to reschedule
Snow on Hallasan can close the 1100 Highland road; the driver-guide will substitute alternative inland stops

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Vehicle and licensed driver-guide; admission and meals pay direct
content: Private vehicle (sedan, SUV, or van based on group size)
Licensed English-speaking driver-guide
Hotel pickup and drop-off anywhere on Jeju
Fuel and tolls for the standard route
All admission fees are not included; pay directly at each venue
Meals are not included
Personal shopping is not included
Driver gratuities are not included

seasonalVariations.name: Spring
seasonalVariations.description: Canola flowers blanket the East coast in late March-April; cherry blossoms peak in early April. Mild temperatures and clear skies make this the most balanced season.

seasonalVariations.name: Summer
seasonalVariations.description: Peak beach season with Hyeopjae and Hamdeok at their strongest; June hydrangeas color the West coast. Expect humidity and possible typhoon weather in August.

seasonalVariations.name: Autumn
seasonalVariations.description: Pink muhly fields peak mid-October to early November; the strongest visibility window for Hallasan and Seongsan views. Often Jeju's best photo season.

seasonalVariations.name: Winter
seasonalVariations.description: Camellia Hill blooms December to February; Hallasan can carry snow on its summit. Cold but visually rewarding, with fewer crowds at all major stops.

bookingTrustItems.title: Licensed local operator
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingTrustItems.title: Route specialists
bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop

bookingSupportSteps.detail: Weather update and any route adjustments

bookingSupportSteps.title: Final Pickup Info
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Exact pickup time and driver contact

bookingSupportSteps.title: Day-of Route Notes
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Morning briefing based on live conditions

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to Jeju (East, West, or South)?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive stops in the area in a single balanced day.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: Moderate walking with some active sections.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — pacing and walking are comfortable for active seniors.

fix_passes.v12: P0+P1 — pickup_dropoff Schema A unification, routeFlowStops type-based, competing_products → internal slugs, anchor_poi_keys aligned
fix_passes.v13: P1.5 — pickup_dropoff Schema A.2 → A.1; practicalAccordionItems / routePhases / seasonalVariations unified to V4 canonical (no Tailwind in data)
fix_passes.v14: P2 — _publication uniform; duration formatting standardized; glanceItems Duration added where missing
fix_passes.v15: P3 round 1 — SEO trim. seo.pageTitle rewritten to ≤65 chars (was 14/25 over 70); seo.metaDescription rewritten to ≤168 chars (was 25/25 over 170). primaryKeywords and ogImage unchanged. Hero/OG images, POI verification, review imports, and routePhases enrichment deferred to later P3 rounds.
fix_passes.v15.2_charter_consolidation: Removed "jeju-popular-sightseeing-private-car-charter-tour" from competing_products per Jason's one-charter-per-region rule (jeju-island-private-car-charter-tour remains as canonical Jeju charter).

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Converted matching_profile scores from 1-5 int scale to 0-1 float scale to match the 3 Step 3 new tours. Mapping 1→0.20, 2→0.40, 3→0.60, 4→0.80, 5→1.00. Mechanical conversion preserves ordinal information; verified-fact recalibration happens in subsequent enhancement batches.

v17_batch7_enhancement.description: Batch 7 deep enhancement — private charter pair (Jeju + Seoul). Both tours have 4 route options as itineraryStops (East/West/South/Custom for Jeju; Gapyeong/DMZ-Paju/Everland-Folk/Custom for Seoul). Each route option enhanced with 1,000+ char descriptions weaving verified facts about constituent POIs into a coherent route narrative. matching_profile recalibrated to verified-fact 0-1 float scores using cross-route weighted averaging. private_tour_fit, customizable_route_fit, flexibility_fit set from None to 1.0. 9 new POIs added to KB (v1.9 → v1.10): udo_island, manjanggul_lava_tube, sanbangsan_mountain, sangumburi_crater, yakcheonsa_temple, garden_of_morning_calm, petite_france, everland, korean_folk_village_yongin. DMZ POIs and Nami Island deferred to Batch 8.

_score_audit._methodology: 0-1 float scale per Phase 1 standard. Both tours are private 9-10hr charters with 4 route options (East/West/South/Custom for Jeju; Gapyeong/DMZ-Paju/Everland-Folk/Custom for Seoul). Scores are weighted averages across the recommended routes, with extreme-outlier dimensions weighted toward the realistic middle (e.g. UNESCO score reflects average across routes — Route 1 maxes UNESCO, but weighted average ~0.65). Charter-format dimensions (private_tour_fit, customizable_route_fit, flexibility_fit) are set to 1.0 by definition. Walking_level and indoor_ratio are time-weighted averages across the typical day.

private_tour_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — Both tours are per-vehicle private charters by definition. Was previously None (unset).

customizable_route_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — Three recommended routes plus a fully custom option. Both tours fully customizable. Was previously None.

flexibility_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — Driver-guide adapts pacing, order, lunch, optional add-ons (Udo ferry, Smart Queue). Was previously None.

small_group_fit.reasoning: Verified — these are PRIVATE charters not small-group. 0.15 reflects that a 1-vehicle-of-9 booking still functions as a small private group, but the product type is private not small_group.

bus_fit.reasoning: Verified — private charter = sedan/SUV/van, never bus. 0.05 reflects near-zero bus fit (only the very largest groups of 10+ would step up to a coach format).

unesco_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED weighted average across routes. Route 1 (East): max UNESCO — Seongsan Ilchulbong is triple-UNESCO + Manjanggul UNESCO 2007. Route 2 (West): minimal UNESCO content. Route 3 (South): partial UNESCO — Jusangjeolli is Global Geopark, Sanbangsan is Geopark. Weighted across routes = ~0.65, significantly lower than the previous 1.0 which assumed Route 1 always selected. Custom Route can max UNESCO if the traveler picks specifically — but the average traveler will not.

scenic_level.reasoning: VERIFIED — all 3 named routes deliver catalog-leading scenic content (volcanic crater, beach, basalt cliff, waterfall, tea field, lava dome). 0.95 calibrated, only marginally below max (saving 1.0 for tours that deliver multiple flagship scenic types in a single 9-hour window — which only Route 1 of 3 does).

first_time_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — strongest first-time-Jeju option because Route 1 alone covers the bucket-list UNESCO sites. 0.95 reflects that the WEST route is less first-time-iconic than East, so an averaged score is just below absolute max.

iconic_landmark_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — Seongsan Ilchulbong is Jeju's single most iconic landmark and is included on Route 1 + Custom. Slight markdown to 0.95 because Routes 2 (West) and 3 (South) trade iconic landmark count for café/temple density.

walking_level.reasoning: VERIFIED — weighted across routes. Route 1: Seongsan summit climb is the bottleneck (~0.7). Route 2: low (~0.3 — beach, park, café). Route 3: Sanbangsan stairs to cave temple (~0.55). Average ~0.5, slightly below previous 0.6.

indoor_ratio.reasoning: VERIFIED — weighted across routes (Fix 1 time-weighted methodology). Route 1: low indoor (~0.15 — Manjanggul is the only major indoor stop, and is currently closed). Route 2: moderate (~0.25 — Hallim Park caves + O'sulloc museum interiors). Route 3: moderate (~0.30 — Yakcheonsa main hall, Olle Market arcade, Sanbanggulsa cave). Average ~0.20, lower than previous 0.30. Note: with Manjanggul closed (until verified reopening), the actual indoor exposure on Route 1 is even lower.

weather_sensitivity.reasoning: VERIFIED — moderate weather sensitivity. Route 1 (East UNESCO) is heavily outdoor and weather-vulnerable. Route 3 (South temples + market) has the strongest indoor fallback. The PRIVATE-charter format itself reduces weather impact (driver-guide can shift to indoor stops on bad-weather days), but the recommended routes are still mostly outdoor. 0.50 calibrated up from 0.40 — previous 0.40 over-credited the charter format flexibility.

shopping_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — only Route 3 has substantial shopping (Olle Maeil Market). Routes 1, 2 have minimal shopping (Aewol cafés, beach souvenir kiosks). Custom Route can max shopping if specifically requested. Weighted ~0.55, materially lower than the previous 0.80 which appeared inflated.

food_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — Jeju's food density is high across all routes (haenyeo seafood, Hallabong tangerines, black pork, Olle Market). Route 1 has the strongest meal hubs (Seongsan harbor); Route 3 has Olle Market. 0.70 calibrated up from previous 0.60.

premium_fit.reasoning: Verified — private vehicle + flexible itinerary + driver-guide = top of premium scale. Maintained at 1.0.

value_for_money_fit.reasoning: Verified — per-vehicle pricing is excellent value for groups of 4-9 but expensive for solo travelers. Slight markdown to 0.55 reflects the per-person break-even calculation: vs. small-group bus tours at ~70,000 KRW per person, the charter only beats it for groups of 5+.

storytelling_fit.reasoning: Verified — driver-guide narrates throughout, and the routes carry strong narrative arcs (UNESCO geology, K-drama, Joseon folk culture, tea-merchant heritage). 0.85 reflects that narrative depth varies by driver-guide assigned and by route chosen — was previously 1.0 which assumed uniformly maximal storytelling, which is not realistic.
